title: pulmonaria auriculata
intro: Pulmonaria auriculata, also known as the ear-leaved lungwort, is a perennial flowering plant native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and expectorant qualities, which can help relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing stress and anxiety, improving sleep quality, and even exhibiting antimicrobial properties. Pulmonaria auriculata is commonly prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to take advantage of its medicinal and health benefits.
